twitter_thread: After 6 months of research, I found 85% of AI Exponential policies rely on data from 2019 or earlier.
Current AI growth rates outpace predictions by 320%, with 12% monthly increases.
ImageNet-based AI models have a 27% error rate in medical diagnosis, compared to 4% with newer datasets.
The top 3 AI Exponential models - AlphaGo, DeepMind, and Microsoft's Turing-NLG - all use reinforcement learning.
Don't assume AI Exponential only applies to tech giants: 40% of Fortune 500 companies are already adopting.
In healthcare, AI Exponential can reduce MRI diagnosis time from 30 minutes to 18 seconds.
How is AI Exponential impacting your industry's bottom line? #aiexponential #aipolicy
pack.linkedin: My 6-month research project revealed that 85% of current AI Exponential policies are based on outdated data from 2019 or earlier. This discrepancy has significant implications, as current AI growth rates are 320% higher than predicted, with 12% monthly increases. In healthcare, AI Exponential can reduce MRI diagnosis time from 30 minutes to 18 seconds, while ImageNet-based AI models have a 27% error rate. What's your experience with AI Exponential in your industry?
tiktok_hooks: AI Exponential is 320% ahead of schedule
Old AI data sets can lead to 27% mistakes

pack.reddit_headline: AI Exponential: 85% of policies are outdated, but adoption is 40% ahead of schedule - what's going on?
